response.justification: While this is a strong senior engineer with 17 years of professional experience and excellent software craftsmanship practices, the candidate lacks the mandatory technical requirements for this specialized role. With only 6 months of documented C++ experience at National Instruments (far below the required 5-10 years), no demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle expertise, minimal Windows platform specialization, and no fixed-income or capital markets domain experience, the gap is too substantial. The career trajectory has consistently moved away from C++ toward web/backend and open-source stacks.
strengths: Demonstrated senior-level software engineering maturity with 17 years of professional experience and strong emphasis on testing, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship
Proven ability to learn new domains and technologies across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure systems
Excellent communication and teaching abilities, evidenced by workshops, talks, and mentorship activities
concerns: Severely insufficient C++ experience (~6 months documented vs. 5-10 years required); career has consistently moved toward TypeScript/Node.js rather than C++
No demonstrated expertise with SQL Server, Oracle, or complex financial databases; background is primarily web/NoSQL infrastructure
Complete absence of fixed-income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge; only vague reference to 'investment practices' at RIDE Capital

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (0.5 years vs 5-10 required)
